title: quercus × tharpii
intro: Quercus × tharpii, also known as the Tharp's oak, is a rare hybrid of two different oak species, Quercus gambelii and Quercus grisea, found primarily in the southwestern United States. The bark and leaves of the Tharp's oak have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Quercus × tharpii typically involve the bark being dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being.
